title: Math — Percentages
description: SAT-style percent problems: percent change, chained percents, percent-of relationships, depreciation, and reverse percent.

questions.prompt: 37% of the items in a box are green. Of those, 37% are also rectangular. Of the green rectangular items, 42% are also metal. Which of the following is closest to the percentage of the items in the box that are not rectangular green metal items?
questions.rationale: Rectangular green metal fraction = 0.37·0.37·0.42 = 0.057498 = 5.7498%. So the percent that are not rectangular green metal is 100% − 5.7498% = 94.2502% ≈ 94.25%.

questions.prompt: Which expression represents the result of increasing a positive quantity w by 43%?
questions.rationale: Increasing by 43% means multiply by 1 + 0.43 = 1.43, giving 1.43w.

questions.prompt: 140 is p% greater than 10. What is the value of p?
questions.rationale: If 140 is p% greater than 10, then 140 = 10(1 + p/100). Divide by 10: 14 = 1 + p/100, so p/100 = 13 and p = 1300.

questions.prompt: The positive number a is 2,241% of the sum of the positive numbers b and c, and b is 83% of c. What percent of b is a?
The number a is 70% less than the positive number b. The number c is 60% greater than a. The number c is how many times b?
questions.rationale: 70% less than b means a = (1 − 0.70)b = 0.30b. Then 60% greater than a means c = (1 + 0.60)a = 1.60(0.30b) = 0.48b.

questions.prompt: The regular price of a shirt at a store is $11.70. The sale price of the shirt is 80% less than the regular price, and the sale price is 30% greater than the store's cost for the shirt. What was the store's cost, in dollars, for the shirt? (Disregard the $ sign when entering your answer.)
questions.rationale: Sale price is 80% less than 11.70, so sale = 0.20(11.70) = 2.34. If sale is 30% greater than cost, then 2.34 = 1.30·cost, so cost = 2.34/1.30 = 1.80.

questions.prompt: Thomas installed a new stove in his restaurant. At the time of installation, the stove had a value of $800. Thomas estimates that each year the value of the stove will depreciate by 20% of the previous year’s estimated value. What is the estimated value of the stove exactly 2 years after Thomas installed it?
questions.rationale: Each year the stove keeps 80% of its value. After 2 years: 800(0.8)^2 = 800(0.64) = 512.

questions.prompt: The number a is 190% greater than the number b. The number b is 80% less than 24. What is the value of a?
The result of increasing the quantity x by 400% is 60. What is the value of x?
questions.rationale: Increasing by 400% means new value = x + 4x = 5x. So 5x = 60 and x = 12.

questions.prompt: The number w is 110% greater than the number z. The number z is 55% less than 50. What is the value of w?
